model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Under a fixed 256-step budget, enable LR decay and increase throughput per step. A modest LR increase plus cosine schedule and larger batch should improve early-training progress without adding architectural complexity.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100","old":"    total_steps = steps_per_epoch * 100","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The cosine schedule currently decays over an overly long horizon, so LR barely changes within the 256-step checker budget. Matching scheduler horizon to fixed-step budget should improve within-budget optimization.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    total_steps = steps_per_epoch * 100","old":"    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The fixed-step cosine horizon degraded validation loss, so revert to the previous longer horizon that produced the best observed result.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With nonzero weight decay, decoupled AdamW often improves generalization versus Adam under short fixed-step training while keeping optimization behavior similar.","secondary_modes":["caching","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After switching to AdamW, a moderate LR increase can improve progress within the short fixed-step budget while staying in a stable range.","secondary_modes":["indexing","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Performance improved strongly when increasing LR to 1.5e-3, so the next high-value probe is a further moderate increase to test whether the short-budget optimum is higher.","secondary_modes":["indexing","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Loss kept improving as LR increased, so test the next incremental step up to locate the short-budget optimum before trying orthogonal changes.","secondary_modes":["indexing","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Gains are tapering but still positive, so one more LR increment is a low-complexity way to check if the optimum is slightly higher under the fixed-step regime.","secondary_modes":["indexing","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"3e-3 regressed, so move back to the best-performing nearby LR before exploring other axes.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With only 256 steps, slightly reducing weight decay can improve fit while keeping AdamW regularization, potentially lowering validation loss near the current LR optimum.","secondary_modes":["indexing","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Lowering weight decay worsened validation loss, so revert to the better regularization setting before further exploration.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Under fixed-step evaluation, increasing batch size raises examples seen per step and can improve short-horizon convergence; this is a simple high-impact throughput probe.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Larger batch size regressed validation loss and increased wall time, so revert to the previously best-performing batch size.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Current best appears optimization-tuned; a modest head-capacity increase can reduce underfitting with minimal architectural complexity and often helps once LR/optimizer are near-optimal.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"After increasing classifier capacity helped, the next logical capacity probe is a moderate width increase in convolutional features to reduce representational bottlenecks.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"With wider convolutional features now helping substantially, increasing classifier head capacity is a simple complementary capacity boost that can further reduce underfitting within the fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The larger classifier head regressed loss, so revert to the better-performing head width before using the remaining budget on other high-value tweaks.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After increasing model capacity, the optimal LR can shift upward; testing a slightly higher LR is a high-leverage probe with minimal complexity.","secondary_modes":["layout","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Higher LR strongly regressed with the wider model, so revert to the previously best-performing learning rate.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The strongest gains came from increasing model capacity, so the final high-upside probe is one more moderate width increase in convolutional channels.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}
